ocr.text: The Water Treatment Metrics for Covanta Holdings during the fiscal year 2024 detail various throughput analyses across
multiple dimensions. The Total Capacity for FY2024 is outlined as 150MLiters, with quarterly capacities of 160MLiters in Q1,
165MLiters in Q2, 170MLiters in Q3, and a decrease to 145MLiters in Q4, culminating in an Annual Total of 160MLiters,
reflecting an Average of 5% with a Percentage Change of 90% from the previous year’s Efficiency Ratio, which serves as a
benchmark at 150MLiters. The Cost per Liter is reported at $0.15 for FY2024, with Q1 at $0.14, Q2 at $0.16, Q3 at $0.13, and
Q4 back to $0.15, achieving an Annual Total of $0.14, with an Average decrease of 2% compared to the Benchmark of $0.15
and a Forecast of $0.16, further contextualized by Notes related to Energy Consumption.

Metric  FY2024  Q1  Q2  Q3  Q4  AnnualTotal  Average  PercentageChange  Benchmark  Forecast  Notes
TotalCapacity  150MLiters  160MLiters  165MLiters  170MLiters  145MLiters  160MLiters  5%  150MLiters  150MLiters  EfficiencyRatio  90%
CostperLiter  $0.15  $0.14  $0.16  $0.13  $0.15  $0.14  -2%  $0.15  $0.16  EnergyConsumption  120kWh/Ml
115kWh/Ml  118kWh/Ml  122kWh/Ml  119kWh/Ml  118kWh/Ml  1%  120kWh/Ml  125kWh/Ml  WastewaterRecycled  75%  78%  80%
Energy Consumption metrics indicate a steady usage of 120kWh/Ml, with quarterly values of 115kWh/Ml in Q1, 118kWh/Ml
in Q2, 122kWh/Ml in Q3, and 119kWh/Ml in Q4, achieving an Annual Total of 118kWh/Ml, an Average change of 1% against a
Benchmark of 120kWh/Ml, while the Forecast anticipates a rise to 125kWh/Ml; Notes reference the importance of Wastewater
Recycled, which stands at 75% overall, with quarterly figures of 78% in Q1, 80% in Q2, and a slight decline to 77% in Q3 and
76% in Q4, leading to a total of 77% for the year, indicating a Percentage Change of 2% against a Benchmark of 75%, with an
anticipated target of 80%. The Greenhouse Gas Index has values of 0.8 for FY2024, 0.75 in Q1, 0.76 in Q2, 0.79 in Q3, and
0.77 in Q4, resulting in an Annual Total of 0.77, with the Average indicating a decline of 2% against a Benchmark of 0.8. The
Compliance Ratio remains a critical metric to ensure adherence to environmental regulations, with all figures above meeting the
necessary standards. This thorough analysis of the Water Treatment Metrics not only provides a comprehensive overview of
operational efficiency and environmental impact but also sets the stage for strategic planning and improvement in future fiscal
periods.
